publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 149-152. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. Greig came in a great fuss this morning, when I was only half-dressed, urgent to see me. So I had to see him as I was, & was ordered to write to you by today's post.-- It is about your accepting the Chair-man-ship of the Surrey Quarter Sessions.

Greig thinks it of the utmost importance to the interests of the County altogether, & says you would have a unanimous election.-- He is most anxious on the subject, & has had a long conversation with Nicholson [two words crossed out] about it . I only repeat to you what I am told to say, as exactly as I can. And I think I have now communicated the pith of it.--
I have had all sorts of people today, & have been tending towards the arrangement of all my plans, & correspondence, &c for the country.
Wheatstone promises to come & see me-- soon at Ockham.-- Everything is going on well I believe & as I could wish it.--
But how much I owe my Dearest Mate for his generous & wise encouragement of all that is best for me & which
tends most effectually to tranquillize & fill up that capacious mind & spirit of the Phoneix's!
Tomorrow the Norwood expedition is to take place, I believe, at 1/2 past one o'clock; & if it is a fine day, it will be agreeable.--
We were disappointed of our Mesmerism today. Williamson is very unwell.
I do not know that I have much more of note to say; or much more time to say anything.--

I am sorry to find that I have 2 more teeth in a very bad way. A visit to Lauzelle this morning resulted in this discovery. He says it is the extreme susceptibility & irritability of the constitution, which causes this continually progressing mischief.-- I
remember your kissing & caressing the Avis before ou went this morning; for which, (altho' very sleepy), I was much obliged.--
